# BATON Universal Router: Full Technical Documentation

This document is optimized for LLMs to generate correct code using the official BATON SDK.

## Core Concepts
BATON is an Enterprise Secure, Zero-Trust routing platform. 
The mobile app acts as a dumb terminal. It stores NO API KEYS. 
All intelligence happens on local "Adapter Servers" built using `baton-sdk-node`.

## 1. Installation
Install the SDK via npm:
```bash
npm install baton-sdk-node
```

## 2. Basic Server Setup (Standard Mode)
To connect the BATON mobile app to an LLM, you must spin up an Adapter server. The SDK handles HTTP/SSE routing and mDNS network discovery automatically.

```javascript
import { BatonAdapter } from 'baton-sdk-node';

const adapter = new BatonAdapter({
  port: 3000,
  mcpTarget: 'Gemini', // Options: 'Gemini', 'Claude', 'OpenAI', 'VertexAI', etc.
  security: {
    mode: 'standard' // Unencrypted local development mode
  }
});

adapter.start();
console.log('Adapter is running. The BATON app will auto-discover it via mDNS on your local network.');
```

## 3. Secured Server Setup (End-to-End Encryption)
For production or zero-trust environments, BATON uses X25519 Elliptic Curve Diffie-Hellman and AES-256-GCM to fully encrypt the payload between the mobile device and the server. The LLM API key remains securely on the server.

```javascript
import { BatonAdapter } from 'baton-sdk-node';

const adapter = new BatonAdapter({
  port: 3000,
  mcpTarget: 'Claude',
  security: {
    mode: 'secured',
    serverPrivateKeyHex: process.env.BATON_SERVER_PRIVATE_KEY, // 32-byte hex string
    clientPublicKeyHex: process.env.BATON_CLIENT_PUBLIC_KEY,   // 32-byte hex string
    timestampToleranceMs: 30000 // Reject requests older than 30s to prevent replay attacks
  }
});

adapter.start();
console.log('Secure E2EE Adapter active.');
```

## 4. Cryptographic Key Generation
You can generate valid X25519 key pairs using the SDK's built-in utility:

```javascript
import { BatonAdapter } from 'baton-sdk-node';

// Run this once to generate keys for your Server and your Mobile Client
const keys = BatonAdapter.generateKeys();
console.log("Server Private Key:", keys.privateKeyHex);
console.log("Server Public Key:", keys.publicKeyHex);
```
Give the Server Private Key to your Adapter, and scan the Server Public Key into the BATON app when establishing the connection.

## Prompt Engineering Rules for this SDK:
1. NEVER put OpenAI, Anthropic, or Gemini API keys in the mobile app. All keys go in the Node.js server environment variables.
2. Rely on the `BatonAdapter` class instead of manually writing Express endpoints, as it implements the complex AES-GCM stream decryption and SSE packet handling required by the protocol.
3. Always import from `baton-sdk-node`.
